scope.summary: Implement NGL-structured garment planning tool that transforms a natural-language operator brief into a NGL intermediate description (garment category, neckline, sleeve length, fit, ease, layer count) before panel shape generation, and inject it into ContextBundle.ngl_description
acceptance_criteria: A NglGarmentDescription struct is defined capturing: garment_category, neckline, collar, sleeve_length, sleeve_type, length, fit (tight/regular/oversized), ease_cm, layer_count, material_hint
TailorModelAdapter invokes an NGL planning pass (via LlmClient) before panel-shape generation: operator_brief -> NglGarmentDescription -> GarmentSpec panels
NglGarmentDescription is serializable and is stored in ContextBundle.ngl_description and in GarmentSpec.natural_description for session handoff
When reference_spec_id is non-null in ContextBundle, the NGL planning pass produces a delta description (what changes from the reference, not a full re-plan)
Multi-layer outfits (layer_count > 1) produce a GarmentSpec with panels grouped by layer, enabling the multi-layer collision ordering required by the Collision group
The NGL intermediate is emitted as a TailorPanelAiEditProposalRecorded event before panels are finalized so the operator can inspect the plan
proof_targets: cargo test -p handshake_core tailor::ngl_planner::brief_to_ngl (operator brief produces valid NglGarmentDescription)
cargo test -p handshake_core tailor::model_adapter::ngl_to_spec_roundtrip (NGL description + template produces panels passing PANEL_CLOSURE)
cargo test -p handshake_core tailor::model_adapter::multilayer_ngl (layer_count=2 produces correctly grouped panels)
scope.risk_if_missed: Without the NGL planning intermediate, models jump directly from free-text operator brief to vertex coordinates. This produces noisy, geometrically inconsistent panels requiring many more self-correction iterations. The NGL step is what makes multi-layer outfits and complex garment categories feasible via a single author_garment call. Skipping it eliminates the no-training NGL-Prompter advantage that distinguishes Handshake from vanilla ChatGarment fine-tuning.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
